name: Paolo
title: Full Professor of Information Processing Systems
interests: Artificial Intelligence; Machine Learning; Big Data Analytics; Medical Image and Video Analysis
short_bio: Leads Arco Lab across artificial intelligence, machine learning, biomedical engineering, and translational medical research.
bio: Paolo Soda graduated with honors in Biomedical Engineering in 2004 and earned a PhD in Biomedical Engineering in 2008, both at Campus Bio-Medico University of Rome. He is Full Professor of Information Processing Systems at UCBM. His research focuses on artificial intelligence, machine learning, big data analytics, and 2D and 3D image and video processing and analysis.

name: Valerio
title: Fixed-term Researcher in Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems
interests: Multimodal Deep Learning; Generative AI; Medical Imaging; AI for Healthcare
short_bio: Works on multimodal deep learning, generative AI, medical imaging, and biomedical applications of artificial intelligence.
bio: Valerio Guarrasi is a fixed-term researcher at Campus Bio-Medico University of Rome in the area of Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems. His work focuses on multimodal deep learning, generative AI, medical imaging, and biomedical applications of artificial intelligence. At UCBM he is also involved in teaching activities in Biomedical Engineering and Intelligent Systems Engineering.

name: Daniele
title: National PhD Candidate in Artificial Intelligence
interests: Generative AI; Multimodal Learning; Medical Data Analysis
short_bio: Works on multimodal artificial intelligence, generative models, and medical data analysis, with a focus on clinically grounded machine learning for healthcare applications.
bio: Daniele Molino is a researcher at Arco Lab, Campus Bio-Medico University of Rome, where he works on artificial intelligence for medicine. His research focuses on multimodal learning, generative models, and data-driven methods for medical imaging and clinical data analysis. His work aims to develop robust and clinically meaningful AI systems that support prediction, interpretation, and decision-making in healthcare settings.

name: Ermanno
title: Assistant Professor in Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems
interests: Federated Learning; Radiomics; Pathomics; Precision Medicine
short_bio: Works on federated learning, radiomics, pathomics, and clinically grounded machine learning for biomedical applications.
bio: Ermanno Cordelli is Assistant Professor in the Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems area at Campus Bio-Medico University of Rome. His work spans artificial intelligence, machine learning, radiomics, pathomics, and precision medicine, with a focus on clinically relevant biomedical applications.

name: Camillo Maria
title: Postdoctoral Researcher in Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems
interests: Multimodal Learning; Missing Data; Clinical Prediction; Transformers
short_bio: Works on multimodal learning, missing data modelling, and transformer-based approaches for clinical prediction.
bio: Camillo Maria Caruso is a postdoctoral researcher affiliated with the Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems area at Campus Bio-Medico University of Rome. His current work focuses on multimodal learning, transformer-based modelling, and data-driven approaches for incomplete healthcare data and clinical prediction.

name: Filippo
title: National PhD Candidate in Artificial Intelligence
interests: Medical Imaging; Prognosis Prediction; Clinical AI; Foundation Models
short_bio: Works on medical imaging, prognosis prediction, and clinically grounded artificial intelligence.
bio: Filippo Ruffini is a PhD candidate affiliated with the Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems area at Campus Bio-Medico University of Rome. His research focuses on medical imaging, prognosis prediction, multimodal modelling, and AI methods designed for clinically meaningful decision support.

name: Aurora
title: National PhD Candidate in Artificial Intelligence
interests: Breast Imaging; Generative AI; Mammography; Multimodal Learning
short_bio: Works on breast imaging, generative AI, and multimodal methods for medical imaging applications.
bio: Aurora Rofena is a PhD candidate affiliated with the Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems area at Campus Bio-Medico University of Rome. Her work focuses on breast imaging, generative AI, and multimodal learning, with particular interest in mammography and clinically relevant imaging workflows.

name: Matteo
title: Researcher in Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems
interests: Medical Imaging; Radiotherapy; Clinical Prediction; Digital Health
short_bio: Works on medical imaging, radiotherapy-related AI, and data-driven clinical modelling.
bio: Matteo Tortora is a researcher affiliated with the Artificial Intelligence and Computing Systems area at Campus Bio-Medico University of Rome. His research spans medical imaging, radiotherapy, clinical prediction, and data-driven methods for healthcare applications.
